jokes.text: In the event of an exact tie, the seat is awarded to the male candidate, and the female candidate is put in jail.
jokes.setup: Explaining official election procedures in a formal tone
jokes.punchline: Absurdly sexist and illegal 'city law' delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist bureaucratic humor - takes the mundane election procedure setup and delivers a completely insane but formally stated rule. Perfect Pawnee government satire.

jokes.text: I don't think it would hold up in court but it is city law.
jokes.setup: Previous absurd statement about jailing female candidates
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that the law is unconstitutional but shrugging acceptance
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that makes the absurdity even funnier by treating it as a minor procedural concern.

jokes.text: Shouldn't we be rewarding our citizens for voting? I'm never against anyone getting a candy bar.
jokes.setup: Discussing voting machine irregularities
jokes.punchline: Bobby's childlike focus on candy rewards instead of election integrity
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Bobby's character - wealthy, naive, missing the point entirely while being accidentally endearing.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I just don't see the problem.
jokes.setup: Voting machine clearly malfunctioning and showing bias
jokes.punchline: Bobby's complete obliviousness to obvious election fraud
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bobby's disconnect from reality, though predictable for his character.

jokes.text: This isn't a bagel.
jokes.setup: Talking about election party details
jokes.punchline: Random complaint about food that has nothing to do with the conversation
jokes.explanation: Mild Tom character moment - his tendency to focus on luxury/food complaints at inappropriate times.

jokes.text: It is no longer a 'Pimps and Hoes' pajama party.
jokes.setup: Describing the election party plans
jokes.punchline: Revealing the original deeply inappropriate party theme
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom inappropriateness - the casual mention of the terrible original theme is perfectly cringe.

jokes.text: That idea was nixed by a certain uptight nerd who shall remain nameless.
jokes.setup: Explaining why the party theme changed
jokes.punchline: Obviously referring to Ben while pretending to be discrete
jokes.explanation: Good Tom petulance and the 'shall remain nameless' while making it obvious who he means.

jokes.text: Me, Drake, and the T-Mobile girl were playing baccarat on a private jet. Ann Perkins walks up to me, and she says, 'Tommy, tomorrow night, I'm taking you back.'
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his 'premonition' dream
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific celebrity cameos in what's supposed to be a romantic prophecy
jokes.explanation: Great Tom delusion - mixing genuine romantic hope with his obsession with celebrity culture and luxury.

jokes.text: Then, Blue Ivy Carter high-fived me and gave me $40 million.
jokes.setup: Continuing the ridiculous dream description
jokes.punchline: Even more absurd detail involving a baby giving him millions
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that makes the dream even more ridiculous while staying true to Tom's character obsessions.

jokes.text: Leslie, you can't vote for yourself, I don't think. I'm pretty sure that's illegal.
jokes.setup: Bobby saying he'll vote for Leslie
jokes.punchline: Bobby's fundamental misunderstanding of how voting works
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bobby moment - his wealthy privilege has left him so disconnected he doesn't understand basic democratic processes.

jokes.text: Yeah. That sounds great, but the Pacers called, and they're asking me to be their starting point guard.
jokes.setup: Being offered a serious political job
jokes.punchline: Ben's sarcastic deflection about an obviously impossible sports career
jokes.explanation: Good Ben humor - using absurd comparison to deflect when overwhelmed by a serious opportunity.

jokes.text: Look at that. Look at that thing. What is that? Don't touch it.
jokes.setup: Characters examining something mysterious
jokes.punchline: Andy's childlike curiosity and April's protective response
jokes.explanation: Cute character moment showing their dynamic but not a strong standalone joke.

jokes.text: Recently, in a moment of weakness, I had sex with Bobby Newport's campaign manager, Jennifer Barkley. Seriously? Yes, several times. And then, several more times. And then a couple more times. And then one more time.
jokes.setup: Chris confessing to a one-night stand
jokes.punchline: The escalating count revealing it was much more than 'a moment'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure - each 'and then' makes it funnier and more awkward, perfect Chris oversharing.

jokes.text: How long has this been going on? Oh, It's just last night.
jokes.setup: Ann assuming it's a long affair based on the count
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was all in one incredibly active night
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection payoff - the audience assumes the same thing Ann does, making the reveal even funnier.

jokes.text: But our ferocious sexual decathlon did radically improve my mood
jokes.setup: Describing his night with Jen
jokes.punchline: Chris's characteristic over-the-top athletic metaphors for sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris Traeger line - his fitness obsession applied inappropriately to sex with characteristic enthusiasm.

jokes.text: April? Got your message! Where are you? Under the table. Why are you hiding?
jokes.setup: Andy looking for April
jokes.punchline: April literally hiding under a table like a child
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy and character moment - April's response to crisis is childlike hiding.

jokes.text: First of all, you did the right thing by hiding under this table. Secondly, your man is here. I'm going to take care of this for us.
jokes.setup: April panicking about deleting files
jokes.punchline: Andy treating hiding under a table as a reasonable adult response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy - supportive but completely missing that hiding isn't actually helpful, treating it as tactical.

jokes.text: I've been playing Xbox for years. I'm really good at fixing it when it freezes. I know exactly what to do.
jokes.setup: April has deleted all the computer files
jokes.punchline: Andy's completely irrelevant Xbox expertise being applied to IT crisis
jokes.explanation: Excellent Andy logic - his childlike confidence that video game skills translate to professional IT problems.

jokes.text: Leslie, I can't figure this thing out. Can you help me? Yeah. Just hang on a second, Bobby. I got ink all over my hands, and the pen thingy came off the chain.
jokes.setup: Bobby asking for help with voting
jokes.punchline: Bobby can't figure out basic paper ballot and broke the pen
jokes.explanation: Good Bobby helplessness - can't handle the most basic civic duty despite being a candidate.

jokes.text: With just 1% of the precincts reporting, adult film star, Brandi Maxxxx, has a commanding lead.
jokes.setup: Early election results being reported
jokes.punchline: The porn star is winning the city council race
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escalation - takes the election seriously but introduces completely unexpected third candidate.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. That's it. It's not Bobby Newport who's going to beat me. It's Brandi Maxxxx. The porn star. What is this, Italy?
jokes.setup: Leslie realizes the porn star is winning
jokes.punchline: Compares Pawnee to Italy (Berlusconi reference)
jokes.explanation: Smart political reference and good Leslie panic mode. Italy reference is clever and topical.

jokes.text: your ambiguous ethnic blend perfectly represents the dream of the American melting pot
jokes.setup: Leslie praising Ann
jokes.punchline: Awkward racial commentary delivered as compliment
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie awkwardness - well-meaning but completely inappropriate compliment about race.

jokes.text: Ron? I handed out 1,000 leaflets today. That is a personal best. Now, I'm off to vote. The polls are closed.
jokes.setup: Jerry proudly announcing his campaign work
jokes.punchline: Classic Jerry failure - worked hard all day but missed voting entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry moment - his enthusiasm and competence undermined by basic incompetence at the crucial moment.

jokes.text: Nothing you can do. Except hope Leslie doesn't lose by one vote.
jokes.setup: Jerry realizing he can't vote
jokes.punchline: Ron's brutal assessment of how Jerry's incompetence could decide the election
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron deadpan - matter-of-factly pointing out the worst possible consequence of Jerry's mistake.

jokes.text: There were moments that I didn't fully understand some of the physical shapes that we were making. But the sensations were amazing.
jokes.setup: Chris describing his night with Jen
jokes.punchline: His fitness-focused analytical approach to sexual positions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Chris characterization - treating sex like a workout with his characteristic analytical enthusiasm.

jokes.text: I'll quit, and we'll leave together. I'm serious. Move to a new city, change our names, burn our fingertips off with acid, swap faces.
jokes.setup: Andy comforting April about potentially getting fired
jokes.punchline: Plan escalates from reasonable to completely insane criminal activities
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from sweet romantic gesture to Face/Off criminal activity. Perfect Andy logic - completely sincere about insane plan.

jokes.text: How long was that? Twenty-two seconds. Boy, that's disappointing.
jokes.setup: Leslie finishing boxing workout feeling exhausted
jokes.punchline: Revealing she only exercised for 22 seconds
jokes.explanation: Great setup/punchline with perfect timing - Leslie's self-awareness about her fitness level is endearing.

jokes.text: Ann, my hands. Don't panic. Here. My hands, Ann. They're in these round gloves.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to read election results
jokes.punchline: Can't function with boxing gloves still on
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy follow-up - Leslie's panic makes her forget she's wearing boxing gloves.

jokes.text: Clear alcohols are for rich women on diets.
jokes.setup: Bartender offering gin and tonic
jokes.punchline: Ron's blunt assessment of clear alcohol with classist/sexist undertones
jokes.explanation: Iconic Ron line - perfectly captures his masculinity obsession and disdain for pretense in one economical phrase.

jokes.text: Well, I've never been one for meeting new people or doing new things or eating new types of food or traveling outside of Southern Indiana. I've had the same haircut since 1978, and I've driven the same car since 1991. I've used the same wooden comb for three decades. I have one bowl.
jokes.setup: Ben asking for relationship advice about change
jokes.punchline: Ron's extreme resistance to any change escalating to absurd specifics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron character moment - escalating from reasonable to absurdly extreme resistance to change, with perfect details.

jokes.text: I still get my milk delivered by horse.
jokes.setup: Ron's list of unchanging habits
jokes.punchline: Reveals he lives completely outside modern society
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation capstone - takes Ron's resistance to change to completely absurd extreme that's somehow believable for him.

jokes.text: Why are you laughing? Because my dream is dead.
jokes.setup: Leslie hearing she lost the election
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate laughter as response to devastating news
jokes.explanation: Great dark comedy moment - Leslie's complete breakdown manifesting as inappropriate laughter.

jokes.text: I'll give you Joe Biden's home phone number.
jokes.setup: Trying to convince Leslie to concede
jokes.punchline: Offering impossibly valuable political contact as bribe
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jen's high-level connections and Leslie's political obsessions in one offer.

jokes.text: Now, hang on. That's interesting.
jokes.setup: Joe Biden phone number offer
jokes.punchline: Ben immediately considers the political value
jokes.explanation: Good Ben political nerd moment - immediately recognizes the value.

jokes.text: I picked the wrong week to do a juice cleanse.
jokes.setup: Election stress continuing
jokes.punchline: Tom's focus on lifestyle trends during major crisis
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character moment - his self-centered focus on health trends during friend's crisis.

jokes.text: Every five-minute coffee-break at work. Every night, every weekend, every spare moment of their lives, they gave to me. If I lose, I'll never forgive myself.
jokes.setup: Leslie reflecting on her friends' sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Her typical self-blame and melodrama
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent but not particularly funny - more dramatic than comedic.

jokes.text: Let's go back to the party. You drive. I've had 11 whiskeys.
jokes.setup: Ron and Leslie finishing serious conversation
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of dangerous level of drinking
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron - treating excessive drinking as matter-of-fact practical consideration.

jokes.text: A dental hygienist who's also a medium, so I can clean people's teeth and tell them when they're going to die.
jokes.setup: Andy asking about dream jobs
jokes.punchline: April's characteristically morbid career combination
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - combining mundane job with her death obsession in perfectly dark but practical way.

jokes.text: Fixed it. What? What? How? Jerry accidentally deletes things all the time. Ten years ago, I installed my own secret backup.
jokes.setup: April panicking about deleting all files
jokes.punchline: Donna prepared for Jerry's incompetence with secret backup system
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff showing Donna's competence and Jerry's established incompetence history.

jokes.text: Where did you get a Washington Monument figurine at midnight on a Tuesday? From my office. I have, like, 50 of them.
jokes.setup: Leslie getting thoughtful gift
jokes.punchline: Leslie's office is full of Washington Monument figurines
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie character detail - her political obsession extends to hoarding government memorabilia.

jokes.text: But you won. You won, Leslie. You won by 21 votes.
jokes.setup: Leslie thinks she lost the recount
jokes.punchline: Ann reveals she actually won
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - reverses the expected outcome for emotional payoff.

jokes.text: That was really sneaky, Ann. I know. I couldn't resist.
jokes.setup: Ann appearing to deliver bad news about election
jokes.punchline: Ann reveals she was pranking Leslie about losing
jokes.explanation: Mild misdirection - Ann showing rare playful side but the setup wasn't very convincing.

jokes.text: Because guess who's got two thumbs and was just cleared from insurance fraud? This guy. You know? Got off on a technicality.
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio asking about job opening
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to insurance fraud while applying for government job
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jean-Ralphio - his characteristic criminal behavior delivered with complete confidence as qualification.

jokes.text: Tommy! This party was amazing. You're amazing. You are ridiculous, but I like you. And I think that we should date all of it.
jokes.setup: Tom's prediction about getting back with Ann
jokes.punchline: Ann drunkenly confirming Tom's 'premonition' came true
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to Tom's dream prediction, though the payoff is predictable drunk confession.

jokes.text: Great win. Great night. Great year. Hot wife. Jackpot. Good job, me.
jokes.setup: Chris reflecting on the night
jokes.punchline: His characteristic self-congratulation taken to narcissistic extreme
jokes.explanation: Good Chris character moment - his positive attitude becoming absurd self-congratulation.

jokes.text: How does this sound? 'Officer Dwyer, Pawnee P.D.'
jokes.setup: Andy realizing April's dream jobs were all police work
jokes.punchline: Andy considering becoming a cop himself
jokes.explanation: Nice setup for future character development - Andy finding his calling through April's influence.

jokes.text: Jerry forgot to vote. Damn it, Jerry!
jokes.setup: Episode ending on high note
jokes.punchline: Reminder of Jerry's crucial mistake that could have changed everything
jokes.explanation: Good callback ending that brings Jerry's mistake full circle as the episode concludes.

jokes.text: City Council, bitches!
jokes.setup: Leslie announcing victory celebration plans
jokes.punchline: Uncharacteristically crude victory exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Leslie's rare use of profanity for celebration.
